connectionRules.triggerSource: Exactly 1 relay output from 1 device (controller or standalone detector). Cannot be wired to multiple sources.
connectionRules.powerSource: 24V DC from system power or dedicated supply

connectionRules.triggerSource: Exactly 1 relay output from 1 device (controller or standalone detector). Cannot be wired to multiple sources.
connectionRules.powerSource: 24V DC from system power or dedicated supply

connectionRules.triggerSource: Exactly 1 relay output from 1 device (controller or standalone detector). Cannot be wired to multiple sources.
connectionRules.powerSource: 24V DC from system power or dedicated supply

connectionRules.detectorConnection: 4-20mA analog only (10 input channels). MIDI connects via J1 analog output → GC10 analog input.
connectionRules.modbusPort: Output to BMS only — NOT for detector input. RS-485 Modbus sends GC10 data to Building Management System.
connectionRules.alertConnection: Controller relays drive alerts centrally. 1 beacon on Relay 1, 1 siren on Relay 2 (typical). Relays are configurable.
connectionRules.alertsNeeded: 1 beacon + 1 siren connected to controller relays (centralized, regardless of detector count)

connectionRules.alertConnection: OEM sensor — no relay outputs, alerts managed by host system via Modbus.

connectionRules.connectionToController: 4-20mA analog (MIDI J1 analog output → GC10 analog input channel)
connectionRules.connectionToGC10: 4-20mA analog ONLY — MIDI J1 output wired to GC10 analog input. Never via Modbus.
connectionRules.modbusUsage: Direct to BMS (standalone/without GC10) or for service/monitoring only — NOT used when MIDI is connected to GC10
connectionRules.alertConnection: Each MIDI relay drives exactly 1 alert device. In standalone mode: 1 alert on Warning relay + 1 alert on Alarm relay. With controller: MIDI relays typically unused for alerts (controller manages centrally).
connectionRules.powerAdapterRequired: When site voltage is 230V AC (MIDI needs 24V)

connectionRules.alertConnection: Each RM relay drives exactly 1 alert device. In standalone mode: 1 alert on Relay 1 + 1 alert on Relay 2. With controller: RM relays typically unused for alerts (controller manages centrally).
connectionRules.powerAdapterRequired: When site voltage is 230V AC (RM needs 12-24V)

connectionRules.needed: When site power is 230V and detectors require 24V

connectionRules.triggerSource: Exactly 1 relay output from 1 device (controller or standalone detector). Cannot be wired to multiple sources.

connectionRules.sensorConnection: Direct mount or remote highway cable
connectionRules.alertConnection: Transmitter relays drive alerts. 1 fault relay + 2 alarm relays.

A.name: Direct-connected sensors only
A.note: Gland and plugs included with transmitter

wiringTopologies.name: MIDI Standalone
wiringTopologies.description: GLACIAR MIDI operates independently with built-in relays for warning and alarm. No controller needed. Power: 24V AC/DC. Each unit has 2 relay outputs (warning + alarm) for direct alert device control.

wiringTopologies.name: MIDI + GC10 Centralized
wiringTopologies.description: Up to 10 GLACIAR MIDI detectors connected to one GLACIAR Controller 10 via 4-20mA analog signals. Controller manages centralized alarm logic, relay outputs, and BMS communication via Modbus.

wiringTopologies.name: X5 Config A — Direct Sensors Only
wiringTopologies.description: X5 Transmitter with 1-2 direct-mount sensor modules plugged into ports A and B. No remote cable. ATEX-rated configuration. Ideal for machinery rooms.

wiringTopologies.name: X5 Config B — Remote Sensors Only
wiringTopologies.description: X5 Transmitter with 1-2 remote sensors connected via cable (up to 300m). Sensors placed in hazardous areas, transmitter in safe area. ATEX-rated.

wiringTopologies.name: X5 Config C — Mixed Direct + Remote
wiringTopologies.description: X5 Transmitter with one direct sensor on port A and one remote sensor on port B (or vice versa). Combines proximity and distant monitoring in one unit.

wiringTopologies.name: X5 + GC10 Centralized
wiringTopologies.description: Multiple X5 Transmitters connected to GLACIAR Controller 10 via 4-20mA analog signals. Controller provides centralized alarm management for large installations.
